thig6.5:1.4: I was the daughter of Majjha, the treasurer.
thig6.5:2.2: rich merchants’ sons longed for me.
thig6.5:2.3: One of them sent my father a messenger, saying,
thig6.5:3.3: I will give in return eight times her weight
thig6.5:4.1: But I, having seen the One Self-awakened,
thig6.5:5.1: He, Gotama, from sympathy, taught me the Dhamma.
thig6.5:5.3: And as I sat in that very seat,
thig6.5:5.4: I attained the third fruit [of non-return.]
thig6.5:6.1: Then I cut off my hair,
thig6.5:6.4: since I made craving wither away.
